secondary_archetype: Customer Success Manager (people manager / enterprise growth lead)

level_signals: 2+ years of experience leading teams in Customer Success, Account Management, or a similar customer growth function
5+ years in customer-facing roles at a B2B SaaS company
Hire, develop, and lead a team of Enterprise Growth Strategists

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years of experience leading teams in Customer Success, Account Management, or a similar customer growth function
5+ years in customer-facing roles (CSM, Account Manager, etc.) at a B2B SaaS company
A track record of driving revenue growth, expansion, and retention
A passion for coaching and developing others — you get energy from seeing your team thrive
Exceptional relationship-building and communication skills, across both customers and internal teams
The ability to simplify complexity — finding creative solutions and guiding your team through ambiguity
A love of systems thinking — identifying bottlenecks, building scalable processes, and driving continuous improvement
A growth mindset and comfort with fast-paced, entrepreneurial environments

scope.surface: Enterprise Growth Strategy (Customer Success) team — helping enterprise customers unlock growth using Clay
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, PLG + enterprise motion, $100M+ ARR
core_responsibilities: Hire, develop, and lead a team of Enterprise Growth Strategists who embody Clay's values — curiosity, creativity, and customer obsession.
Foster a culture of learning, accountability, and excellence, where experimentation is encouraged and insights are shared freely.
Motivate your team to build deep, trusted partnerships with customers, helping them define success and deliver measurable value.
Own key customer relationships yourself — setting the bar for engagement, advocacy, and strategic influence.
Serve as an escalation point and problem-solver, helping turn challenges into opportunities for stronger collaboration.
Partner cross-functionally with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Marketing to represent the voice of the customer and shape our roadmap around their needs.
Forecast, track, and report on KPIs tied to expansion, retention, and customer health — using data to tell a story and guide decisions.
Identify opportunities to innovate and scale our approach to enterprise success — designing playbooks, frameworks, and strategies that amplify impact.

green_flags: Company has crossed $100M in ARR and raised a $100M Series C — strong financial foundation
Clay's $5B valuation with employee tender offers signals liquidity options for employees
Explicit mission and operating principles communicated — cultural transparency
Community of 11,000+ customers provides a large enterprise customer base to work with
Notable enterprise customers listed (Anthropic, Notion, Google, Ramp) — credibility signal
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — candidates cannot assess fit without this
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information provided
No team size or org structure details given, making scope ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from management requirements and years of experience thresholds)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type set to 'unclear' — JD does not mention location, remote, or hybrid policy
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no comp data stated or inferable
ambiguities: Location/work arrangement is entirely absent from the JD — could be remote, hybrid, or onsite; cannot determine without additional information.
The minimum years threshold: '5+ years in customer-facing roles' subsumes the '2+ years leading teams' — it is unclear if these are meant to be additive or overlapping.

All bullets in 'What You'll Bring' are phrased as 'you might be a great fit if you have:' which introduces softening language, yet they read as standard must-haves. Treated as hard requirements given unconditional phrasing within each bullet.
